programmes.show_description: Austin Healey Lenham Six/VW Beetle: Dave and Jack head to East Yorkshire to pick up a dusty Alfa Romeo and a 1930s Jowett. But Dave's more taken with a Carry On-style, bum-wobbling exercise machine. S
Austin 7 Chummy/Triumph TR4A: Derek picks up a lovingly restored 1925 Calthorpe motorbike that he thinks is more of a work of art than anything ever produced by Picasso.
Fiat And The Fascists: Find out how Fiat didn't just make cars: they made trains, they made planes, and like modern-day kingmakers, they made and broke governments too.
Shopping from Home.
Dunbar to Edinburgh: Michael Portillo travels around the British Isles by train and learns about the challenges the coastal regions face, starting at Siccar Point in Berwickshire.
Leith to Culross: Michael Portillo continues his Scottish coastal journey along the Firth of Forth at the railway engineering icon for which it's famous, the Forth Bridge.
Stirling to East Neuk: Michael Portillo begins this leg of his Scottish railway journey from Dunbar to Peterhead in the beautiful medieval city of Stirling.
St Andrews to Stonehaven: Michael Portillo visits Leuchars, known nowadays on the railways as Leuchars for St Andrews, which is Scotland's oldest university.
Volvo P1800/Morris Milk Float: Dave gets wind of a possible free lunch at the officers' mess when he heads off to collect a stylish Swedish Volvo P1800S from an RAF base in the Midlands.
Ford Escort Mexico: Derek has sold everything from trucks to bicycles - but he can't remember the last time he auctioned off a two-seater plane.
The People's Car: While America had Ford or Chrysler or Buick, Hitler also wanted a car that would transform his nation: the 'people's car'- a Volkswagen.
Rudolf Hess: Dramatisation of the trial of Hitler's deputy, Rudolf Hess, who was declared insane by the Fuehrer after making an unexpected errand of peace to Britain in 1941.
World War I Special: Fiona Bruce presents from Etaples Military Cemetery in France, as the team discovers the personal stories behind treasured objects associated with WWI.
Compliation (1): Fiona Bruce looks back at favourite finds from the past 40 years of the programme, while a selection of experts reveal what happened next to some memorable items.

Lotus Esprit/Land Rover: Derek heads to County Durham where Neil Walker is reluctantly letting his late dad's car collection go, including a Japanese take on a classic British Jag; the Mitsuoka Viewt.
Ford Fiesta/Malton Forest Rally: Dave collects a Ford Fiesta XR2i that's not moved from a garage for twenty-one years and Derek picks up a 1956 Siva Tourer, made famous in Doctor Who.
The People's Car: While America had Ford or Chrysler or Buick, Hitler also wanted a car that would transform his nation: the 'people's car'- a Volkswagen.
Rudolf Hess: Dramatisation of the trial of Hitler's deputy, Rudolf Hess, who was declared insane by the Fuehrer after making an unexpected errand of peace to Britain in 1941.
World War I Special: Fiona Bruce presents from Etaples Military Cemetery in France, as the team discovers the personal stories behind treasured objects associated with WWI.
Simon Reeve returns to visit one of Britain's favourite counties to discover what life is really like for the locals after the summer crowds have gone.
Rolls Royce Phantom/VW Bettle: Derek's charmed by a 1929 Rolls Royce Phantom with an unusual history, involving a WW1 hero and a tale of fraud.
Jeff recreates an historic flight in search of vital clues, before an archive discovery drops a bombshell that causes the team to question everything.

Lotus Esprit/Land Rover: Derek heads to County Durham where Neil Walker is reluctantly letting his late dad's car collection go, including a Japanese take on a classic British Jag; the Mitsuoka Viewt.
Ford Fiesta/Malton Forest Rally: Dave collects a Ford Fiesta XR2i that's not moved from a garage for twenty-one years and Derek picks up a 1956 Siva Tourer, made famous in Doctor Who.
Volvo P1800/Morris Milk Float: Dave gets wind of a possible free lunch at the officers' mess when he heads off to collect a stylish Swedish Volvo P1800S from an RAF base in the Midlands.
Ford Escort Mexico: Derek has sold everything from trucks to bicycles - but he can't remember the last time he auctioned off a two-seater plane.
The People's Car: While America had Ford or Chrysler or Buick, Hitler also wanted a car that would transform his nation: the 'people's car'- a Volkswagen.
Shopping from Home.
Forvie to Peterhead: Michael Portillo's Scottish railway journey reaches Aberdeen, from where he heads out to Forvie National Nature Reserve.
Inverness to the Cairngorms: Michael Portillo crosses the Culloden Viaduct aboard the Caledonian Sleeper to arrive bright and early in Inverness.
Cullen to Cawdor: Michael Portillo travels by train north from Inverness to the Orkney Islands. Beginning on the Moray Firth, Michael admires the Cullen Viaduct.
Invergordon to Tain: Michael Portillo's Scottish coastal railway journey reaches Invergordon on the Cromarty Firth, the third great inlet on the northeast coast of the Highlands.
Sunbeam Rapier: 100 motorbikes come in for auction from every era of the 20th century. Also, the team assess a 1965 Vanden Plas Princess with a four litre Rolls Royce engine.
Fiat 500: A rusty 1970 Fiat 500 goes under the hammer - Derek reckons it's the kind of vehicle that is so iconic that it has to be saved.
Apocalypse Factories: The incredible story of the arms race to create America's nuclear arms factories.
Politics: Dramatised documentary examining how Adolf Hitler and the Nazi party seized power in Germany during the 1930s and led to the destruction of democracy in the country.
Eltham Palace: Fiona Bruce presents from Eltham Palace in South London, where finds include a collection of Gerry and Sylvia Anderson puppets.
Erddig 1: Fiona Bruce and the team of antiques experts visit Erddig, north Wales, where items include a dazzling emerald and diamond ring worn by the owner's grandmother on the Titanic.
Robin Reliant/Triumphs/Bantam: Dave meets the inspirational owner of a Reliant Robin who's selling it to raise money for charity.
Land Rover/Dino/Morris 8/Saab 9-5: Dave Mathewson has put his best clean T-shirt on to collect a 1965 Land Rover from a stately home - and it turns out that the trusty car has a real pedigree.
Apocalypse Factories: The incredible story of the arms race to create America's nuclear arms factories.
Politics: Dramatised documentary examining how Adolf Hitler and the Nazi party seized power in Germany during the 1930s and led to the destruction of democracy in the country.
Eltham Palace: Fiona Bruce presents from Eltham Palace in South London, where finds include a collection of Gerry and Sylvia Anderson puppets.
Simon Reeve begins an ambitious journey travelling the entire length of the Americas - in this first leg, Simon travels from Alaska and down through Canada to Vancouver.
An Almighty Bang: More wartime laughs in the saucy comedy set in occupied France. The Colonel tries to stop Von Flockenstoffen's kidnap plan. Fanny and LeClerc are stuck in a folding bed.
Fleeing Monks: Another trip to the French cafe where the owner is always, 'ow you say, 'aving it away!' Sentenced to death by the Resistance, Rene tries to flee to England.
Up the Crick Without a Piddle: More French farce. Rene and Edith meet an old friend and are awarded medals by Churchill after being transported to England in place of the airmen.
Robin Reliant/Triumphs/Bantam: Dave meets the inspirational owner of a Reliant Robin who's selling it to raise money for charity.
Land Rover/Dino/Morris 8/Saab 9-5: Dave Mathewson has put his best clean T-shirt on to collect a 1965 Land Rover from a stately home - and it turns out that the trusty car has a real pedigree.
